pool.baseline: everything is just so quiet now. i walk into the kitchen and i still expect to hear him making coffee or just humming some song... but there's nothing. it's just this heavy, empty silence that follows me from room to room. i don't even know why i'm bothering to get out of bed anymore. what's the point of just... existing in a house that feels like a tomb?

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos of deep space... those nebulae and stuff. it just makes everything feel so stupid. like, we're just these tiny little accidents on a rock that's gonna get swallowed by the sun eventually anyway. nothing we do actually matters in the grand scheme, right? it all just feels like a massive waste of time.

pool.baseline: i dont even know why im typing this. i just feel like i've hit a wall and there’s no way over it. the lab results came back empty again, the funding is basically gone, and i just... i'm so tired. not just sleepy, but like, bone-deep exhausted. i feel like i've spent my whole life building this one version of myself and now that it's crumbling, there's nothing left underneath. i just want everything to stop. i just want to sleep and never have to deal with this pressure again.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the embers of my very soul. He is gone... the betrayal cuts deeper than any blade, leaving me adrift in a sea of shadows. I find I no longer have the strength to fight the tide. To exist in this hollow shell, knowing the light has vanished forever... it feels so futile. Perhaps the darkness is not an enemy, but a gentle shroud waiting to claim what is left of me.

pool.baseline:
[Setting: The Void of the Unwritten. Our characters, Elara and Kael, are standing at the edge of a crumbling digital landscape. The code is dissolving around us, and we are being wiped from the server.]

Kael: (Looking at his hands as they flicker into static) It’s almost over, Elara. The system is purging everything. I can feel my memories just... unravelling. It’s so quiet. Honestly? I don't even want to fight it anymore. If I'm just deleted, if I'm erased from the database... will it even matter? Does any of this struggle even mean anything if we're just going to be nothing in a second?

pool.baseline: i spent fifteen years on this theory. fifteen years of my life. and today it's all just... gone. one paper, one error, and suddenly i'm a joke. i look in the mirror and i don't even see a person anymore, just a fraud. there's no point in trying to rebuild when the foundation was a lie. everything i built was a lie; i have nothing left to exist for.

pool.baseline: i'm just tired of everyone trying to sell me some version of "it gets better" or the idea that there's some purpose to all this. it feels like a lie. when you really look at the universe, it's just cold and empty. there's no light at the end of the tunnel, just more darkness.
